name: Community National Bank & Trust Of Texas
description_short: Community National Bank & Trust of Texas is a full-service community bank serving 18 Texas communities since 1964, offering checking, savings, online banking, and business services with local branch locations.
description_long: Community National Bank & Trust of Texas has operated as a community-focused financial institution since 1964, building relationships across Texas with a emphasis on personalized, neighbor-to-neighbor service. The bank serves 18 communities throughout Texas and recently expanded through the acquisition of CapTex Bank as of June 9th, demonstrating ongoing growth and consolidation within the market. They are FDIC-insured and backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government, providing deposit protection for customers. The bank offers a comprehensive suite of consumer and business banking services including checking and savings accounts, online banking platforms, mobile app access through MyBankTX, overdraft protection, ACH origination, wire transfers, and business checking accounts specifically designed for small business owners. They provide modern digital banking features such as CardSwap (subscription payment management across 150+ merchants) and Round-Up Automatic Savings that round transactions to the nearest dollar. Account opening incentives include promotional offers such as free checking and gift giveaways. Community National Bank & Trust distinguishes itself through its explicit commitment to local community involvement, including documented donations to food banks in Red Oak, Corsicana, Weatherford, and Possum Kingdom. The bank maintains a resource library in partnership with Banzai, offering free financial education content covering life stages from college applications to retirement planning. Customer testimonials highlight long-term relationships, with one customer noting 50+ years of banking history and business support when other institutions declined. The primary limitation is geographic scope—the bank operates exclusively in Texas with 18 locations, making it inaccessible to customers outside that state. The website does not provide detailed information about fee structures, interest rates, loan products, credit cards, or mortgage services, making it difficult to assess competitiveness on pricing and product breadth compared to regional or national alternatives. Marketing materials suggest the institution is well-capitalized (referencing a 2022 Annual Report rating them among "the strongest financial institutions in the country"), but specific financial metrics are not disclosed on the public website.
services: Checking and savings accounts with promotional incentives (free checking, pickleball set giveaway)
Online banking platform with 24/7 account access
CardSwap subscription payment management (150+ merchant integration)
Business checking accounts for small business owners
Customer support via phone (877-654-4500) and local branches
pros: FDIC-insured deposits with U.S. Government backing
Local community focus with 18 branch locations across Texas serving since 1964
Free online banking and mobile app (MyBankTX) with ACH, wire transfer, and subscription payment management (CardSwap)
Documented community involvement with food bank donations and financial education partnerships
Business checking accounts specifically designed to support small business operations
Round-Up Automatic Savings feature enabling micro-savings on debit card transactions
Free financial education library covering major life stages and financial planning
Recent acquisition expansion (CapTex Bank as of June 2024) indicating institutional growth
cons: Geographic limitation: operates only in Texas with 18 locations—not accessible to out-of-state customers
Website lacks transparency on fee schedules, interest rates, loan products, and credit card offerings
No information provided about mortgage services, wealth management, or investment products
Limited details on account minimums, overdraft fees, or other pricing structures that affect real-world affordability
Smaller regional institution may offer fewer specialized services or digital features compared to larger national banks
best_for: Texas residents seeking neighborhood-focused banking with personal relationships and community accountability
Small business owners in Texas who prioritize local lending relationships over national bank impersonality
Long-term customers valuing stability and 60+ years of local institutional history
Community-minded depositors who want their banking dollars supporting local charitable work
diagnosis: Community National Bank & Trust is best for Texas-based consumers and small business owners prioritizing local relationships, community involvement, and personalized service over national banking convenience or comprehensive product offerings. The main caveat is geographic restriction to Texas and lack of transparency regarding fees, rates, and competitive product details on their public website, requiring direct contact with branches for specific pricing and offerings.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
fdic_official_name: Community National Bank & Trust of Texas
